War For Bramnunea

Branunea Secondus — Faith, Soil, Sand, and Survival



Branunea Secondus is a world of contrasts — a planet where fertile heartlands, ancient forests, and vast deserts coexist in uneasy balance, shaped by millennia of settlement, exploitation, and devotion. Located above the Great Rift in Imperium Nihilus, the world stands isolated from the wider Imperium, cut off from the Astronomican’s guiding light yet defiantly loyal to the Emperor. Its four great provinces — the Northern Province, the Central Frontier, the Western Marches, and the Great Eastern Desert — form a tapestry of cultures, landscapes, and histories that together define the character of the world.
The Northern Province is the administrative and ecclesiastical heart of Branunea Secondus, home to the capital hive‑city of Viridianus Magna and the grand basilica that houses the incorrupt body of Saint Biffobrin. Its fertile coasts and ancient highlands form the political and spiritual core of the planet, where the Governor and Cardinal‑Prefect rule in uneasy harmony. To the south lies the Central Frontier, the planet’s bread‑road — a region of rolling plains, river valleys, and hilly scrublands that feed the world. Here stands Saint’s End, the site of the saint’s martyrdom and one of the most sacred places on the planet.
To the west, the world becomes wild. The Western Marches are a land of storm‑lashed coasts, dense primeval forests, and the industrial hive‑city of the Valley of Judgement. Once, the entire planet was covered in jungle like this, but centuries of industrial strip‑harvesting have reduced the forests to a fraction of their former glory. The Marches remain a frontier of danger and opportunity — a place where lumber barons, nomads, and predators all carve out their existence beneath the canopy.
To the east lies the planet’s greatest expanse: the Great Eastern Desert, a vast wasteland of salt flats, iron‑rich barrens, towering plateaux, and shifting dune seas. Regions like the Praetorian Flats and Ashen Expanse are among the harshest environments on the planet, while the Aurex Plateaux stands as a sacred monolith rising from the sands. Mining enclaves, nomadic tribes, and pilgrimage routes cling to survival in this unforgiving land, where the past lies buried beneath the dunes.



After the Rift




The opening of the Cicatrix Maledictum changed Branunea Secondus forever. Warp‑charged auroras now dominate the night sky, weather patterns have become erratic, and strange phenomena plague every province. Astropathic communication is unreliable, Administratum oversight has ceased, and the world has been forced into self‑reliance. Yet the people endure. The Northern Province has become a fortress of faith, its basilica a beacon in the dark. The Central Frontier feeds the world with renewed urgency, its farmers working by torchlight beneath warp‑lit skies. The Western Marches stand as a shield against the unknown, their forests growing darker and stranger with each passing year. And the Great Eastern Desert has become a place of omens — a shifting frontier where reality itself seems thin.
In the age of Nihilus, Branunea Secondus is a world defined not by its isolation, but by its resilience. Cut off from the Imperium yet unwavering in its loyalty, it stands as a testament to the Emperor’s enduring light — a frontier world that refuses to fall, even as the galaxy burns around it.
